name: Rags to Riches
description: A disadvantaged protagonist rises to success, status, or fulfillment through perseverance, talent, luck, or moral worth.

nodes.label: Lowly State
nodes.definition: The protagonist exists in a condition of poverty, obscurity, or oppression. The gap between their potential and their circumstances is visible to the audience if not to the world.
entry_conditions: Audience sees the character's disadvantage clearly
exit_conditions: An opportunity or catalyst appears that makes change possible
typical_variants: Orphan in hostile household
Talented person trapped by class
Decent person ground down by circumstance
failure_modes: Protagonist's lowliness is told but not felt
No hint of inner quality that justifies eventual rise
signals_in_text: Small acts of dignity or skill

nodes.label: Opportunity Appears
nodes.definition: A chance event, benefactor, or discovery opens a path out of the lowly state. The protagonist may not yet recognize its significance.
exit_conditions: The protagonist engages with the opportunity
typical_variants: Invitation from a powerful figure
Discovery of a hidden talent or inheritance
Lucky break that provides access to a new world
failure_modes: Opportunity is so large it removes all challenge
Opportunity has no connection to the character's qualities

nodes.label: Initial Rise
nodes.definition: The protagonist enters a new social or material world, experiencing early success or acceptance. The contrast with the lowly state is sharp.
exit_conditions: The protagonist has tasted success but has not yet been tested
typical_variants: First public recognition
Entry into a higher social circle
Early financial or professional success
failure_modes: Rise is too smooth — no friction or doubt
The character adapts instantly with no disorientation

nodes.label: Social Trials
nodes.definition: The protagonist faces challenges from the new world: jealousy, tests of worthiness, cultural friction, or exposure of their origins.
entry_conditions: Others in the new world resist or test the protagonist
exit_conditions: Protagonist has navigated enough challenges to reach a position of apparent security
typical_variants: Rival who resents the newcomer
Social test the protagonist nearly fails
Betrayal by a supposed ally
failure_modes: Trials are trivial and never threaten the rise

nodes.label: Apparent Peak
nodes.definition: The protagonist reaches what appears to be the summit of success. This is a false resolution — the character has risen but has not yet been fully tested.
entry_conditions: External success is at its height
exit_conditions: A crisis reveals that the rise is incomplete or built on fragile ground
typical_variants: Public triumph
Winning the prize or the love interest
Maximum wealth or status
failure_modes: Peak feels like the real ending — no tension remains
No seeds of the coming crisis are planted earlier
signals_in_text: Brief moment of everything going right

nodes.label: Crisis of Worth
nodes.definition: The protagonist's position is threatened — their origins are exposed, their luck runs out, or they face a moral test that wealth or status cannot solve.
entry_conditions: A flaw, secret, or external force destabilizes the position
exit_conditions: The protagonist must choose between the trappings of success and their true values
typical_variants: Public exposure of origins
Loss of the benefactor or fortune
Moral dilemma between ambition and integrity
failure_modes: Crisis is resolved by external rescue rather than character choice
Crisis is trivial and the protagonist never truly risks losing what was gained

nodes.label: Proof of Character
nodes.definition: The protagonist demonstrates their true worth through an act that proves their rise was deserved — not by luck or deception, but by genuine quality.
entry_conditions: Crisis has stripped away false supports
The protagonist must act from core values
exit_conditions: The protagonist's moral or personal quality is publicly or privately validated
typical_variants: Selfless act that costs the protagonist their gains
Demonstration of the talent that was always latent
Choosing integrity over advantage
failure_modes: Proof is handed to the character rather than earned
No real cost to proving worth
signals_in_text: Others recognize the character's true nature

nodes.label: True Elevation
nodes.definition: The protagonist achieves a stable, earned position — richer in character than in the apparent peak. The new status reflects who they are, not just what they have.
exit_conditions: A new equilibrium is established that integrates the character's origin and growth
typical_variants: Restored to a higher position with new respect
Modest but dignified success
Recognition by those who matter most
failure_modes: Elevation is purely material with no character dimension
Ending ignores the cost paid during the crisis

edges.label: Chance Arrives
edges.preconditions: Lowly state is fully established; audience understands what the character lacks
edges.effects_on_stakes: Hope enters the story; the possibility of change creates tension
edges.effects_on_character: Character must choose whether to engage with the opportunity
edges.common_alternatives: Opportunity is forced on the character rather than discovered
edges.anti_patterns: Opportunity appears without the character having demonstrated any quality worth rewarding

edges.label: Seizes the Chance
edges.preconditions: Opportunity is present; protagonist acts on it
edges.effects_on_stakes: Leaving the old world behind; new expectations arise
edges.effects_on_character: Identity begins to shift from underdog to contender
edges.common_alternatives: Character is swept into the rise involuntarily
edges.anti_patterns: No cost or risk to seizing the chance

edges.label: New World Pushes Back
edges.preconditions: Character is in the new environment; others notice and react
edges.effects_on_stakes: Success is shown to be conditional, not guaranteed
edges.effects_on_character: Character discovers that rising is harder than arriving
edges.common_alternatives: Trials come from within (self-doubt) rather than without
edges.anti_patterns: No one opposes or tests the character's place in the new world

edges.label: Reaches the Top
edges.preconditions: Trials are navigated; the character has more to lose now
edges.effects_on_stakes: Everything the character has built is now at risk in the coming crisis
edges.common_alternatives: Peak is reached suddenly through a single breakthrough
edges.anti_patterns: Peak feels earned enough to end the story — no instability planted

edges.label: Foundation Cracks
edges.preconditions: Apparent peak is reached; a hidden weakness or external force emerges
edges.effects_on_stakes: Maximum — the protagonist may lose everything gained
edges.effects_on_character: Forced to confront whether the rise was genuine or superficial
edges.common_alternatives: Crisis is triggered by the character's own mistake rather than exposure
edges.anti_patterns: Crisis comes from nowhere with no foreshadowing

edges.label: Chooses Character Over Status
edges.preconditions: Crisis has stripped away external supports; character must act from values
edges.effects_on_stakes: Protagonist risks losing gains to preserve integrity
edges.effects_on_character: True identity is revealed under pressure
edges.common_alternatives: Proof comes through talent display rather than moral choice
edges.anti_patterns: External rescue resolves the crisis without character agency

edges.label: Earns True Place
edges.preconditions: Character has proven their worth; the crisis is resolved
edges.effects_on_stakes: Stakes dissolve into a stable new normal
edges.effects_on_character: Character integrates their origin and their growth into a coherent identity
edges.common_alternatives: Elevation is modest but meaningful rather than grand
edges.anti_patterns: Ending is purely material; character's inner journey is ignored
